prepR4pcm 0.3.0

Compare two reconciliation objects

Source: R/reconcile_diff.R
reconcile_diff.Rd

Identifies what changed between two reconciliation runs by comparing their mapping tables on name_x. Reports species that gained or lost a match, species whose match type changed, and species whose match target changed.

Usage

reconcile_diff(x, y, quiet = FALSE)

Arguments

x

A reconciliation object (the "before" state).

y

A reconciliation object (the "after" state).

quiet

Logical. If TRUE, suppress console output. Default FALSE.

Value

A list with the following components:

gained

Tibble of species matched in y but unresolved in x.

lost

Tibble of species matched in x but unresolved in y.

type_changed

Tibble of species whose match_type differs.

target_changed

Tibble of species whose name_y differs.

summary

A one-row tibble with counts: n_gained, n_lost, n_type_changed, n_target_changed, n_shared.

Examples

data(avonet_subset)
data(tree_jetz)
# Without crosswalk
r1 <- reconcile_tree(avonet_subset, tree_jetz,
                     x_species = "Species1", authority = NULL)
#> ℹ Reconciling 919 data names vs 657 tree tips
#> ℹ Matching 919 x 657 names through 2 stages...
#> ℹ Stage 1/4: Exact matching...
#> ℹ Stage 2/4: Normalised matching (0 matched so far)...
#> ℹ Stage 3/4: Synonym resolution (657 matched so far)...
#> ℹ Stage 4/4: Fuzzy matching (657 matched so far)...
#> ✔ Matched 657/919 data names to tree tips
# With crosswalk overrides
data(crosswalk_birdlife_birdtree)
overrides <- reconcile_crosswalk(crosswalk_birdlife_birdtree,
                                  from_col = "Species1", to_col = "Species3",
                                  match_type_col = "Match.type")
#> ℹ 1933 many-to-one entries (lumps) included
#> ℹ 225 one-to-many entries (splits) included
#> ✔ Crosswalk: 3039 overrides (8079 identical pairs skipped)
r2 <- reconcile_tree(avonet_subset, tree_jetz,
                     x_species = "Species1", authority = NULL,
                     overrides = overrides)
#> ℹ Reconciling 919 data names vs 657 tree tips
#> ℹ Matching 919 x 657 names through 2 stages...
#> ℹ Stage 1/4: Exact matching...
#> ℹ Stage 2/4: Normalised matching (68 matched so far)...
#> ℹ Stage 3/4: Synonym resolution (657 matched so far)...
#> ℹ Stage 4/4: Fuzzy matching (657 matched so far)...
#> ✔ Matched 657/919 data names to tree tips
d <- reconcile_diff(r1, r2)
#> 
#> ── Reconciliation diff ──
#> 
#>   Shared species (in both x and y): 919
#> • Gained matches: 68
#> ! Lost matches: 68
#> • Type changed: 0
#> • Target changed: 0
#> 
#> ── Gained (first 5) ──
#> 
#> ✔ "Calamanthus montanellus" -> "Calamanthus_campestris" (manual)
#> ✔ "Gerygone citrina" -> "Gerygone_flavolateralis" (manual)
#> ✔ "Cracticus argenteus" -> "Cracticus_torquatus" (manual)
#> ✔ "Coracina dobsoni" -> "Coracina_striata" (manual)
#> ✔ "Coracina welchmani" -> "Coracina_caledonica" (manual)
#> ℹ ... and 63 more
#> 
#> ── Lost (first 5) ──
#> 
#> ! "Calamanthus campestris" was "Calamanthus_campestris" (normalized)
#> ! "Gerygone flavolateralis" was "Gerygone_flavolateralis" (normalized)
#> ! "Cracticus torquatus" was "Cracticus_torquatus" (normalized)
#> ! "Coracina caledonica" was "Coracina_caledonica" (normalized)
#> ! "Coracina striata" was "Coracina_striata" (normalized)
#> ℹ ... and 63 more
cat("Gained:", nrow(d$gained), "| Lost:", nrow(d$lost), "\n")
#> Gained: 68 | Lost: 68
